# Maestro v0.1.1

The current Maestro beta release for live MuseScore editing.

## Download

- `Maestro-v0.1.1-macOS-unsigned.dmg`
- `Maestro-v0.1.1-macOS-unsigned.zip`

## Before You Open It

- This macOS build is unsigned and not notarized.
- On first launch, right-click `Maestro.app` and choose `Open`.
- If macOS blocks it again, use `System Settings > Privacy & Security > Open Anyway`.

## What Changed

- Clarified Maestro beta as a macOS desktop companion for live MuseScore editing, not a standalone notation editor.
- Tightened the packaged app so it avoids broad PyInstaller collection of unused scientific and test payloads.
- Added local persistence for non-secret settings plus macOS Keychain storage for an OpenAI API key.
- Added local structured logging and a `Diag` action that copies a sanitized diagnostics bundle for beta feedback.

## Manual Setup

Windows, Linux, and advanced macOS users can keep using the manual source-install flow in the [README](https://github.com/TidalTunes/Maestro/blob/main/README.md).

## Beta / AI Warning

Maestro is experimental. AI-generated output may be incorrect, unsafe, incomplete, unreliable, or musically inappropriate. Review every generated change before using it.

## Credits

Maestro is developed by Kashi Tuteja, Arthur Gilfanov, and Matthew Li, students at Yale University.
